title: Rocket Lab USA, Inc. Form 10-K -- Space Systems segment (SolAero solar power products, reaction wheels, spacecraft battery power systems)
claim_supported: Rocket Lab's FY2025 Form 10-K describes its Space Systems reportable segment (part of $601.8M FY2025 total revenue, vs. $436.2M FY2024 and $244.6M FY2023) as including vertically-integrated space solar cells/CICs/solar-array products (via the Jan 2022 SolAero Technologies Corp. acquisition) plus reaction-wheel flywheels and high-capacity, high-voltage spacecraft batteries -- an incumbent-map data point (vertically-integrated commercial supplier of spacecraft power hardware) not previously in this lane's ledger. NOTE: identity of the primary audited document could not be independently established this session (see notes); treat the specific dollar figures/segment wording as company-reported and not independently confirmed against the primary filing.
locator: Rocket Lab USA, Inc. Form 10-K (FY2025, filed 2026-02-26 per SEC EDGAR acceptance stamp cited by the aggregator), Space Systems segment description, as reproduced via StockTitan SEC-filing summary; primary filing itself inaccessible this session
notes: SCOUT/ORCHESTRATOR FLAG: direct WebFetch of sec.gov 10-K returned HTTP 403; only stocktitan.net third-party aggregator was fetchable. VERIFIER TIER-DISCIPLINE DETERMINATION: Made an aggressive, multi-route re-fetch attempt this session specifically targeting SEC/primary-source identity: (1) sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar company-search -- HTTP 403; (2) data.sec.gov/submissions/CIK0001819994.json (SEC's own machine-readable filing-index API) -- HTTP 403; (3) direct sec.gov/Archives/.../rklb-20251231.htm (the canonical 10-K document itself) -- HTTP 403; (4) efts.sec.gov full-text-search API -- HTTP 403; (5) the underlying 8-K earnings-release exhibit at sec.gov/Archives/.../rklb-ex99_1.htm (located via WebSearch) -- HTTP 403; (6) Rocket Lab's own investor-relations domain investors.rocketlabusa.com -- redirected to investors.rocketlabcorp.com, which then timed out/connection-reset on two separate attempts; (7) Rocket Lab's own official earnings-release PDF at investors.rocketlabcorp.com/node/12026/pdf -- timed out. Every SEC.gov route and every Rocket-Lab-official route failed this session; only the stocktitan.net aggregator returned content, and it explicitly states it does NOT provide a direct hyperlink to the original SEC filing (confirmed via this session's own fetch of that page), which weakens (though does not eliminate) confidence in strict textual fidelity. Independent WebSearch this session found a consistent ~$602M FY2025 revenue figure and matching Space-Systems-segment/SolAero/reaction-wheel description via a separate aggregation path (investors.rocketlabcorp.com press release as surfaced through search), which corroborates the substance of the stocktitan figures but is still not a first-hand fetch of the audited 10-K itself. DECISION: because the audited primary document's identity/text could not be independently established via ANY route this session despite 7 distinct attempts, and per this wave's explicit tier-discipline mandate to resist waving records through as T1, this record is DOWNGRADED from the scout's proposed T1 to T2 (credible, cross-corroborated secondary reproduction of a company filing, not a confirmed-fidelity primary document). Retained as accepted (useful, plausible, non-vendor-marketing incumbent-map data point, internally consistent across two independent observation sessions) rather than rejected outright. Does not count toward the audited-company-filing T1 quota.
